name: Kanazawa
summary: Kanazawa is one of Japan's safest cities with extremely low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. The main risks are natural disasters, particularly earthquakes and tsunamis, as evidenced by the January 2024 earthquake that caused minimal damage in the city but severely impacted the nearby Noto Peninsula.
quickVerdict: Kanazawa offers exceptional safety for tourists with minimal violent crime and petty theft risks. The city maintains Japan's renowned reputation for public order and cleanliness. While natural hazards like earthquakes pose the primary concern - as demonstrated by the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake - the city's infrastructure and emergency systems are well-prepared. Solo female travelers, families, and all demographics can feel secure exploring this cultural gem, though basic earthquake awareness and preparedness are recommended.

neighborhoods.name: Kenrokuen Garden Area
neighborhoods.description: The historic garden district and surrounding areas are extremely safe with heavy tourist presence during the day. Well-lit and patrolled, ideal for all travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Higashi Chaya District
neighborhoods.description: The preserved geisha district offers excellent safety with traditional wooden buildings and well-maintained streets. Popular with tourists and very secure.

neighborhoods.name: Central Kanazawa (Katamachi)
neighborhoods.description: The main commercial and entertainment district with restaurants and bars. Safe overall but use normal urban precautions during late night hours.

neighborhoods.name: Omicho Market Area
neighborhoods.description: Bustling market district that's very safe during business hours with good foot traffic. Watch for pickpockets in crowded areas but violent crime is rare.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station District
neighborhoods.description: Modern area around JR Kanazawa Station with hotels and shops. Very safe with good lighting and security, though can be busy during peak travel times.

neighborhoods.name: Residential Areas
neighborhoods.description: Quiet residential neighborhoods are exceptionally safe with low crime rates. Some areas may be less familiar to tourists but pose no significant safety risks.

scams.name: Overpricing for Foreign Tourists
scams.description: Some restaurants and shops may charge higher prices to foreign visitors, especially in tourist areas. This dual pricing practice is becoming more common.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices beforehand, ask to see a menu, and compare costs at different establishments. Learn basic Japanese numbers to understand pricing.

scams.name: Fake Monk Charity Scam
scams.description: People dressed as Buddhist monks may approach tourists offering trinkets, then demand payment or donations. Real monks don't solicit money this way.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline any unsolicited approaches from people in monk robes. Real monks have donation boxes but don't chase tourists for money.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Scams
scams.description: Rare instances of taxi drivers not using meters or taking longer routes, particularly targeting foreign tourists who may not know the area.
scams.howToAvoid: Always ensure the meter is running when you get in. Use Google Maps to track your route and ask about approximate fare and travel time upfront.

scams.name: ATM Helper Fraud
scams.description: Scammers may approach tourists at ATMs offering help with transactions while secretly attempting to steal card information or watch PIN entry.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept help from strangers at ATMs. Cover your PIN when entering it and use ATMs located inside banks or convenience stores when possible.

soloFemale.overview: Kanazawa is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers with very low rates of harassment or assault. The city maintains Japan's culture of respect and safety, making it one of the world's safest destinations for women traveling alone. Public transportation is safe at all hours, and locals are generally helpful and respectful.
tips: Walking alone at night is generally safe, but stick to well-lit main streets and tourist areas
Use Japan's extensive network of koban (police boxes) if you need assistance or directions
Learn basic Japanese emergency phrases and keep important numbers saved in your phone
Trust your instincts - if something feels wrong, don't hesitate to seek help from authorities or other people

nightSafety.overview: Kanazawa maintains excellent safety standards after dark with well-lit streets and minimal violent crime. The entertainment districts around Katamachi are safe but require normal urban caution. Public transportation runs safely throughout the evening, and emergency services are readily available.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas, especially around the entertainment district
Be cautious around bars and drinking establishments - watch your drinks and never leave them unattended
Keep emergency contact numbers saved in your phone and know your hotel address in Japanese
Use official taxis or public transportation rather than unlicensed vehicles

transport.metro: Kanazawa has limited rail transit with the Hokuriku Shinkansen connecting to Tokyo and the IR Ishikawa Railway for local travel. Services are extremely safe, punctual, and clean with excellent security systems.
transport.rideshare: Rideshare services like Uber are not widely available in Kanazawa. Traditional taxi services are the primary option for on-demand transportation and are very reliable and safe.
transport.taxis: Taxis in Kanazawa are very safe and regulated with professional drivers. Meters are standard and overcharging is rare. Drivers may have limited English but are honest and helpful.
transport.tips: Public transportation is exceptionally safe and punctual. Always validate tickets and follow posted rules. For taxis, ensure the meter is running and have your destination written in Japanese for clarity.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes, traditional restaurants, and some hotels
Bow slightly when greeting people and receiving business cards with both hands
Don't tip - it's not customary in Japan and can cause confusion
Be quiet on public transportation and avoid phone calls
Follow onsen etiquette if visiting hot springs - wash thoroughly before entering baths

health.overview: Japan has world-class healthcare with excellent medical facilities in Kanazawa including Kanazawa University Hospital and Kanazawa Medical University Hospital. Medical standards are very high with advanced technology and well-trained staff.
health.water: Tap water throughout Kanazawa is completely safe to drink and meets high quality standards. Bottled water is widely available but unnecessary for health reasons.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations are required for travel to Japan. Routine vaccinations should be up to date. Consider travel insurance as healthcare costs can be high for foreigners without Japanese insurance.
health.altitude: Kanazawa is at sea level with a temperate climate. Summer can be hot and humid while winters are mild with occasional snow. No altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Tokyo: +81-3-3224-5000 (24-hour emergency line available)

faq.a: Yes, Kanazawa is exceptionally safe for tourists with very low crime rates and excellent infrastructure. It's considered one of Japan's safest cities with minimal risk of violent crime or theft.
Kanazawa is very safe at night with well-lit streets and low crime rates. While violent crime is rare, exercise normal urban caution in entertainment districts and avoid excessive drinking.

faq.q: Is Kanazawa safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Kanazawa is one of the world's safest destinations for solo female travelers with minimal harassment and excellent public safety. Women can comfortably walk alone even at night in most areas.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Kanazawa?
faq.a: There are no particularly dangerous areas in Kanazawa. All neighborhoods are relatively safe, though some residential areas may be less familiar to tourists. Exercise normal caution in busy entertainment districts at night.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Kanazawa?
faq.a: Yes, public transportation in Kanazawa is extremely safe, clean, and reliable. The rail systems and buses operate with excellent safety standards and are comfortable to use at any time of day.
